title: logger.exception may leak ccxt exchange-side error metadata into ops logs
summary: H-1172 introduces logger.exception calls that emit the full traceback for any exception from _fetch_and_price_window or the persist phase. ccxt's exchange-error messages occasionally embed request URLs with signed query parameters or response bodies that include account identifiers. While Fernet InvalidToken is masked upstream by classify_exception, ccxt.ExchangeError subclasses surfacing here are logged unredacted. The audit-trail path keeps the 500-char sanitised summary, but stdout/sentry now receives the raw chain. Impact: low — log sinks are operator-only, not user-visible; ccxt scrubs auth headers; no decrypted secrets reach this scope. Worth confirming sentry's PII scrubbing covers ccxt response bodies.
fix: Replace logger.exception with logger.warning('reconstruct failed allocator=%s key=%s venue=%s kind=%s', allocator_id, api_key_id, venue, type(exc).__name__) and emit exc_info only behind a DEBUG-level guard. Alternatively, log a sanitised short message and rely on sentry's scrubbing to catch the rest — but document the trust boundary explicitly.
